name: Dispatcharr
short_desc: Self-hosted IPTV / M3U / Xtream stream manager and EPG proxy, routed through a self-hosted WireGuard VPN gateway.
author: tenequm
description: Dispatcharr is the *arr-family IPTV cousin: it ingests M3U playlists or Xtream Codes accounts, filters and organises channels, auto-matches EPG, and re-serves everything to Jellyfin/Plex/Emby as a stable HDHomeRun/M3U/XMLTV source. Its proxy engine multiplexes a single upstream connection to many local viewers (respecting provider connection limits) and adds buffering for stream stability. This deployment forces all provider traffic through a WireGuard gateway (gluetun in custom mode) with a hard kill-switch, so the IPTV provider sees the gateway IP and the local ISP cannot see or block the streams. IPTV is pull-only, so no inbound port forwarding is required. Optional /dev/dri passthrough enables Intel QuickSync (VA-API) hardware transcoding for output profiles and DVR.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Private Key
form_fields.hint: Client private key for the VPN gateway tunnel (the peer the gateway is configured to accept).

form_fields.label: WireGuard Server Public Key
form_fields.hint: Public key of the VPN gateway's WireGuard interface.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Endpoint IP
form_fields.hint: Public IP of your VPN gateway.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Endpoint Port
form_fields.hint: UDP port the gateway's WireGuard interface listens on.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Tunnel Address
form_fields.hint: This client's address inside the tunnel. Must be unique among the gateway's peers.

form_fields.label: Allowed LAN / Docker Subnets
form_fields.hint: Comma-separated subnets allowed to bypass the kill-switch (LAN + Runtipi docker network) so the web UI and Jellyfin can reach Dispatcharr.
